statusLine: European fuel stocks are under strain with 17 of 27 countries showing critical levels in at least one fuel type, despite EU averages remaining above the 90-day minimum threshold.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows significant stress across the continent, with critical shortages affecting multiple countries and fuel types. Romania faces the most severe situation with all three fuel categories in critical status, including dangerously low diesel reserves at just 20.2 days. Poland, Spain, Luxembourg, and Germany also show critical levels across all fuel types, indicating widespread supply chain pressures following recent strategic reserve releases.

The data reveals stark regional disparities in fuel preparedness. While EU averages for petrol (98.7 days) and diesel (97.4 days) remain above the mandatory 90-day minimum, jet fuel stocks at 91.1 days sit precariously close to the threshold. Eastern European nations appear particularly vulnerable, with 6 of the 8 lowest-stocked countries located in this region. Conversely, Nordic and Baltic states like Estonia and Slovenia maintain robust reserves, with some categories exceeding 200-300 days.

The current supply situation reflects the ongoing impact of Middle East disruptions, evidenced by Brent crude's recent decline to $90.38 per barrel after significant volatility. Fuel prices remain elevated across the EU, with diesel averaging €2.021 per liter and notable regional variations - Netherlands petrol reaches €2.316 while Malta maintains €1.34. This pricing disparity, combined with uneven stock distributions, suggests continued market stress despite some recent crude price relief.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries show critical fuel stock levels in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, Spain, Luxembourg, and Germany most severely affected across all fuel types
Jet fuel reserves are particularly tight EU-wide at 91.1 days, just above the 90-day mandatory minimum, with 15 countries showing critical or warning levels
Eastern European nations face disproportionate strain, holding 6 of the 8 lowest national stock positions following recent strategic reserve releases
Significant fuel price variations persist across the EU, with Netherlands petrol at €2.316 per liter versus Malta at €1.34, indicating ongoing market disruptions
Brent crude dropped 9.07% to $90.38 per barrel, providing some relief from the ~27% increase since Middle East conflict escalation
